transition_controls: Draft artifacts may enter review only as controlled lifecycle records.
Approved-ready state requires review obligations to be resolved.
Final-ready state requires approval obligations and task closure dependencies to be resolved.
Supersession must identify the replacement lifecycle record.
Supersession must identify the replacement lifecycle record.
Supersession must identify the replacement lifecycle record.
Supersession must identify the replacement lifecycle record.

required_review_obligations.description: Technical review confirms scope, placeholders, limitations, and reviewer attention points remain visible.
Quality review confirms controlled output limitations are preserved before approval readiness.

required_approval_obligations.description: Approval readiness confirms the lifecycle record is prepared for downstream review but does not create signatures or QMS approval.

required_task_closure_dependencies.description: Task closure dependencies must be resolved before final-ready state.

supersession_controls: Superseded lifecycle records must identify the replacement lifecycle record.
Supersession does not delete or rewrite historical renderer artifacts.
limitation_carry_forward_controls: Placeholder presence must carry forward from renderer metadata.
Limitation presence must carry forward from renderer metadata.
Standards warning presence must carry forward from renderer metadata.
rule_controls: Generated prose cannot mutate lifecycle truth.
M29.8 lifecycle state is a controlled record layer over renderer artifact metadata only.
No electronic signatures, eQMS approval, product release, or deployment are implemented.

library_controls: M29.8 implements lifecycle/workflow integration rules only.
Lifecycle records preserve renderer metadata and limitations without making product-ready claims.
Electronic signatures, QMS approval, output validation, UAT, release, deployment, productization, and SaaS readiness remain out of scope.
